claim: Measured human-skin spectral variation has a finite empirical geometry whose dimension and strata dependence can be estimated from ISSA.
The observability of measured skin spectra depends on the alignment between empirical skin variation and the declared camera-illuminant row space.
primary_evidence: ISSA; camera sensitivity family; illuminant family

claim: Physically plausible skin spectra can collapse to indistinguishable camera responses under realistic tolerance, with prevalence and severity that may vary by operating condition.
Tone-dependent uncertainty can be decomposed into interacting measurement and biological mechanisms under a declared forward and noise model.
primary_evidence: ISSA; validated forward model; camera/illuminant/noise families

claim: An added measurement can be selected to contract uncertainty subject to cost and subgroup-parity constraints.
A physics-derived recoverability score has incremental clinical explanatory value beyond lesion contrast and data quality.
